dishes.name: APK IPA
dishes.raw_description: Originally Brewed in Apopka, Florida APK IPA is a balanced India Pale Ale that's not too hoppy and not too bitter.  It has hints of orange making this delicious beer easy to drink on any given day.
dishes.reasoning: Beer is brewed from plant-based ingredients; no animal products mentioned. | evidence: “IPA - American”
dishes.restaurant_name: Three Odd Guys Brewing of Longwood
dishes.address: 956 W State Rd 434, Longwood, FL 32750, USA

dishes.name: Beach Line
dishes.raw_description: Grab your shades. Hit the 528. Pretend traffic does not exist. Beach Line Kölsch is our light and crisp Florida crusher brewed for beach days, poolside hangs, and any afternoon that feels better with your toes in the sand. It is clean, refreshing, and smooth with just enough subtle fruit character to keep things interesting. No overthinking. No heavy bitterness. Just an easy-drinking Kölsch built for sunshine, salt air, and one more round before the cooler runs dry.
dishes.reasoning: Beer is brewed from plant-based ingredients; no animal products mentioned. | evidence: “Kölsch”
dishes.restaurant_name: Three Odd Guys Brewing of Longwood
dishes.address: 956 W State Rd 434, Longwood, FL 32750, USA

dishes.name: Cactus Limeade
dishes.raw_description: Close your eyes. Take a sip. Ignore your inbox. Cactus Limeade is our tropical sour getaway packed with prickly pear, pineapple, and lime. It is bright, tart, juicy, and ridiculously refreshing — the kind of beer that tastes like poolside lounging, beach-road daydreams, and a tiny umbrella that somehow never made it into the glass. No passport required. Just good taste.
dishes.reasoning: Beer is brewed from plant-based ingredients; no animal products mentioned. | evidence: “Sour - Berliner Weisse”
dishes.restaurant_name: Three Odd Guys Brewing of Longwood
dishes.address: 956 W State Rd 434, Longwood, FL 32750, USA

dishes.name: Chief's Horse Feathers
dishes.raw_description: Hail To The Chief!  Chief's Horse Feathers is playfully light, charmingly crisp, deliciously buttery and an all around perfect style Blonde Ale.  It's easy to enjoy and refreshingly honest with light ingredients that make you smile.  It'll have you saying "Awwwww Horse Feathers".  It's a simple beer made great to honor the life and service of son, father, grandfather, husband and friend, Chief Petty Officer - Boatswain Mate Paul "Chief" Ivey.
dishes.reasoning: Beer is brewed from plant-based ingredients; no animal products mentioned. | evidence: “Blonde / Golden Ale”
dishes.restaurant_name: Three Odd Guys Brewing of Longwood
dishes.address: 956 W State Rd 434, Longwood, FL 32750, USA

dishes.name: Czech If It's German Pils
dishes.raw_description: We started with the idea of brewing a traditional Czech-inspired pilsner, but in true Three Odd Guys fashion, we decided to make it our own. Built on our house German lager strain, this beer has the clean, crisp backbone you expect from a classic pilsner. We then gave it a small modern twist with a light dry hop and a kiss of Nelson Sauvin hops. The result is a dry, refreshing, incredibly crushable summer pilsner that bridges old-world lager tradition with just enough Odd personality. Clean. Crisp. Snappy. Made for Florida afternoons.
dishes.reasoning: Beer is brewed from plant-based ingredients; no animal products mentioned. | evidence: “German Pilsner”
dishes.restaurant_name: Three Odd Guys Brewing of Longwood
dishes.address: 956 W State Rd 434, Longwood, FL 32750, USA

dishes.name: Disorder
dishes.raw_description: Life gets messy. Beer should make it better. Disorder Porter leans into the chaos with bold espresso flavor, roasted malt depth, and a smooth dark finish that keeps everything just under control. It is rich without being heavy, roasty without being harsh, and balanced enough to make “just one porter” turn into a second round. Coffee lovers, dark beer fans, and anyone who likes their pint with a little attitude — this one is for you. Embrace the Disorder.
dishes.reasoning: Beer is brewed from plant-based ingredients; no animal products mentioned. | evidence: “Porter - American”
dishes.restaurant_name: Three Odd Guys Brewing of Longwood
dishes.address: 956 W State Rd 434, Longwood, FL 32750, USA

dishes.name: Fence Check Brown Ale
dishes.raw_description: Sometimes, when you're sitting out in the backyard having a few beers, you just have to check the fence.  Fence Check Brown Ale is an easy drinking, non-traditional hop profile, light bodied American Brown Ale.  The creamy, nutty and chocolaty flavors make this Brown sinfully easy to drink.
dishes.reasoning: Beer is brewed from plant-based ingredients; no animal products mentioned. | evidence: “Brown Ale”
dishes.restaurant_name: Three Odd Guys Brewing of Longwood
dishes.address: 956 W State Rd 434, Longwood, FL 32750, USA

dishes.name: Longwood Lager
dishes.raw_description: Longwood Lager is our hometown Helles — smooth, balanced, refreshing, and brewed for the place we are proud to call home. It brings classic German lager tradition together with a little new-school personality, using Hallertau noble hops, Nelson Sauvin hops, German lager yeast, and natural carbonation for a crisp, clean pint with a beautiful foamy head. It is the beer for sunny afternoons, neighborhood hangouts, “just one more” conversations, and anyone who believes a simple beer can still be seriously well made. Longwood, this one's for you!
dishes.reasoning: Beer is brewed from plant-based ingredients; no animal products mentioned. | evidence: “Lager - Witbier”
dishes.restaurant_name: Three Odd Guys Brewing of Longwood
dishes.address: 956 W State Rd 434, Longwood, FL 32750, USA

dishes.name: Masterbearding
dishes.raw_description: A juice bomb with a creamy texture that leaves you wanting more. Super hazy look that is juicy with low bitterness. Showcasing each hops true aroma with a great tropical fruit finish.
dishes.reasoning: Beer is brewed from plant-based ingredients; no animal products mentioned. | evidence: “IPA - New England”

dishes.name: MidKnight Bike Ride
dishes.raw_description: Come with us on a MidKnight Bikeride.  It's a deliciously balanced Imperial / Double that is not too hoppy and not too bitter.  It's just right.  This Malt forward beer, with hints of orange is an easy drinker and will quickly become one of your favorite Double IPAs.  It may even have you hopping on a bicycle around midnight to take a spin to celebrate.

dishes.name: Minty Chocolate With Benefits
dishes.raw_description: You know that little chocolate mint you grab on the way out after dinner? We turned that feeling into a beer. Minty Chocolate With Benefits brings together rich roasted chocolate, smooth porter depth, and a cool kiss of peppermint for a dessert-inspired pour that is bold, balanced, and surprisingly refreshing. It is sweet enough to feel like a treat, roasty enough to stay beer-forward, and minty enough to make your mouth do a little happy dance. A great way to finish the night — or get it started.
dishes.reasoning: Beer is brewed from plant-based ingredients; no animal products mentioned. | evidence: “Porter - American”
dishes.restaurant_name: Three Odd Guys Brewing of Longwood
dishes.address: 956 W State Rd 434, Longwood, FL 32750, USA

dishes.name: Opalite
dishes.raw_description: Happiness is not always complicated. Sometimes it is cold, bubbly, tropical, and glowing orange. Opalite Hard Seltzer brings passion fruit, orange, and guava together in a light, crisp, refreshing pour that tastes like sunshine with a little sparkle. It is full of flavor without being heavy, bright without being too sweet, and easy enough to sip while you decide that today is, in fact, going to be a good one. A little tropical. A little bubbly. A little main-character energy.
dishes.reasoning: Hard seltzer is typically made from fermented sugar and flavorings, no animal products. | evidence: “Hard Seltzer”
dishes.restaurant_name: Three Odd Guys Brewing of Longwood
dishes.address: 956 W State Rd 434, Longwood, FL 32750, USA

dishes.name: Orange Moon Rising
dishes.raw_description: Brewed under the glow of Florida sunsets and full-moon patio nights, Orange Moon Rising is our bright, refreshing take on a Belgian-style witbier. Hazy, smooth, and approachable, it blends soft wheat character with tangy orange, citrusy aroma, Belgian yeast notes, and a subtle hint of coriander spice. With low bitterness and a creamy finish, this is a great intro beer for new craft drinkers while still delivering the classic witbier character that seasoned beer fans appreciate. It is light, refreshing, citrus-forward, and brewed for Florida drinking weather.  Light. Citrusy. Smooth. A little mysterious.
dishes.reasoning: Beer is brewed from plant-based ingredients; no animal products mentioned. | evidence: “Wheat Beer - Witbier”
dishes.restaurant_name: Three Odd Guys Brewing of Longwood
dishes.address: 956 W State Rd 434, Longwood, FL 32750, USA

dishes.name: Purple Mane Pale Ale
dishes.raw_description: Inspired by the purple pride and roaring match day energy of Orlando soccer fans, Purple Mane Pale Ale is brewed for hot Florida days, pre-game pints, and post-win celebrations. This balanced pale ale gives you a little hop character up front without overwhelming the palate, then finishes smooth, clean, and refreshing. It is approachable enough for casual beer drinkers but flavorful enough for pale ale fans looking for something crisp, local, and full of Orlando spirit.
dishes.reasoning: Beer is brewed from plant-based ingredients; no animal products mentioned. | evidence: “Pale Ale - American”
dishes.restaurant_name: Three Odd Guys Brewing of Longwood
dishes.address: 956 W State Rd 434, Longwood, FL 32750, USA

dishes.name: Red Heads Wrath
dishes.raw_description: Redheads have a reputation. This beer does absolutely nothing to calm that down. Red Heads Wrath is bold, smooth, malty, and just fiery enough to keep you honest. It rolls in with caramel sweetness, a little hop bite, fruity character, and a finish that drinks smoother than a 9% beer has any business drinking. It is balanced. It is dangerous. It is probably already plotting your next pour.
dishes.reasoning: Beer is brewed from plant-based ingredients; no animal products mentioned. | evidence: “Red Ale - Imperial”

dishes.address: 956 W State Rd 434, Longwood, FL 32750, USA

dishes.name: Santa's Belly
dishes.raw_description: Santa’s Belly Eggnogg is a festive Winter Warmer brewed with cozy holiday flavor in mind. Smooth, lightly sweet, and warming, it brings notes of eggnog spice, vanilla, and soft malt character in a glass made for cooler nights, ugly sweaters, and holiday cheer. Rich enough to feel seasonal, but easy enough to enjoy more than once, this one brings big belly energy without needing a chimney.

dishes.name: Straight Outta Class
dishes.raw_description: The final bell has rung and the gradebook is closed. Straight Outta Class is a peanut butter and strawberry sour made for teachers ready to trade lesson plans for taproom plans. Bright strawberry jam, smooth peanut butter, and a tart sour finish make this PB&J-inspired beer the perfect first sip of summer break. No more copies. No more grades. No more students throwing shade. Class dismissed.

dishes.name: Sunburnt and Zesty
dishes.raw_description: This is what happens when blood orange meets Florida attitude. Sunburnt and Zesty is light, bubbly, citrusy, and full of smiles — like a beach day in a glass without the sand in your shoes. Blood orange brings a bold, juicy flavor with just enough tartness to keep things refreshing, while the crisp seltzer finish keeps it clean and easy to enjoy. It is the kind of drink made for patio weather, pool days, and pretending you remembered to reapply sunscreen.
